items.question: What age is PrimaSTEM for?
items.reply: From age 4. The board speaks each token - knowing numbers isn't required. Ages 4–7 work with logic and simple programs. Ages 7–10 add mathematics, precise angles, and drawing.

items.question: How does it work?
items.reply: Children place command tokens on a wooden board - that's their program. Press Start - the robot executes. Wrong result? Find the error, swap a token, run again. No screen, no apps.

items.question: What's in the kit?
items.reply: Ladybug robot (wood, USB-C, Bluetooth), control board with 11 dual slots, command tokens (cardboard, NFC, color-coded), and an online teacher's guide with lesson plans.

items.question: Does the robot draw?
items.reply: Yes. Insert a marker and every movement leaves a line. Children program stars, spirals, polygons, fractals. Forward 50, Right 144, Repeat 5 = a star.

items.question: Does it work with maps from other robots?
items.reply: Yes. The default step size is adjustable - 10, 12, 15, 25 cm or any value. PrimaSTEM works with any grid map you already have.

items.question: How much does it cost?
items.reply: We sell wholesale to distributors and don't publish prices online - contact us and we'll connect you with a distributor or send wholesale terms directly. Schools and families: available through our distributors.

items.question: Can I rent instead of buying?
items.reply: Rental is available for schools. Contact us for details and pricing.

items.question: Is there a distributor in my country?
items.reply: We're just starting - early pilots and partners in France, Belgium and Luxembourg, with distributor conversations underway across Europe. Contact us to order directly or to bring PrimaSTEM to your country.

items.question: How long does the battery last?
items.reply: USB-C charging, runs for approximately 2 hours per charge, depending on usage.

items.question: Is it safe for children?
items.reply: CE + UKCA certified. Made from wood. No screen. Bluetooth connection. Recommended for ages 4+ due to token size.

items.question: Can teachers create their own tokens?
items.reply: Yes. Every token is a standard NFC tag (13.56 MHz). Write any code using a free phone app - create your own numbers, loops, or arithmetic operations in under a minute.

items.question: Can my child use it without a teacher?
items.reply: Yes. Lessons are available on the documentation site in 10 languages, suitable for parents. Video tutorials on YouTube.
